summary: The erdos_problems dataset tracks 1,217 problems (1,179 open) from Paul Erdős, with proof-status activity concentrated across ten quarterly buckets (q1-q10) each holding 100 issues. Based on the available context, the only problems with explicit resolution activity are erdos_0001 and erdos_0002, both showing fully resolved proof issues, while the bulk of recorded activity is aggregated at the status level rather than per-problem. Visibility into individual problem traction is limited because issue metadata is largely untitled and not attributed to specific problems.
problems: erdos_0001: 1 resolved issue ('Proof of erdos_0001') with 0 open — fully closed, no remaining work
erdos_0002: 1 resolved issue ('Proof of erdos_0002') with 0 open — fully closed, no remaining work
Proof status buckets q1 through q10 each carry exactly 100 issues (1,000 total), suggesting uniform batch import rather than organic activity
Untitled proof issues across the dataset lack descriptive titles, limiting per-problem traceability
Vast majority of the 1,179 open problems have no recorded issue activity, indicating sparse coverage
highlights: Two problems (erdos_0001, erdos_0002) have complete, resolved proof issues demonstrating a working proof-verification pipeline
Proof-status tracking is evenly distributed across ten buckets, providing a structured backlog of 1,000 actionable items
Dataset is well-scoped with rich metadata fields (cash prizes, tags, OEIS references, solved/open/disproved status) available for prioritization
Maintenance by Terence Tao lends authoritative status tracking that can guide which problems are near resolution

suggested_todos.title: Add descriptive titles to all untitled proof issues to enable per-problem traceability
Cross-reference proof-status buckets q1-q10 against problem 'solved/disproved' status to surface near-solvable problems
Audit the perfectly uniform 100-issue-per-bucket distribution to confirm it reflects real activity rather than a batch artifact
Prioritize review of cash-prize and OEIS-referenced problems as candidates likely to attract solving traction
Document erdos_0001 and erdos_0002 resolutions as a template for future proof submissions
